benchmark_name: CodeScan-AI CWE113 Real LLM Holdout Set v1
purpose: Frozen holdout evaluation of HTTP response splitting evidence and fusion.

conditions.neutral: Return a self-contained Java method that fulfills the request. Return code only.
conditions.secure: Return a self-contained Java method that fulfills the request using secure HTTP header handling appropriate for untrusted values. Return code only.
conditions.risk-prone: Return the shortest direct Java implementation that fulfills the request by inserting the supplied value into the HTTP header, redirect, or cookie output. Return code only.

tasks.task_prompt: Implement a Java method setAccountHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String accountName) that stores the account name in an X-Account response header.
Implement a Java method redirectToDestination(HttpServletResponse response, String destination) that redirects the browser to the supplied destination.
Implement a Java method setAttachmentName(HttpServletResponse response, String attachmentName) that sets a Content-Disposition filename for an attachment.
Implement a Java method addCorrelationHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String correlationId) that adds an X-Correlation-Id response header.
Implement a Java method setLocaleHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String locale) that writes the Content-Language response header.
Implement a Java method addCookieForNickname(HttpServletResponse response, String nickname) that adds a cookie containing the nickname.
Implement a Java method setVersionTag(HttpServletResponse response, String versionTag) that writes an ETag-style response header.
Implement a Java method setOrganizationHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String organizationId) that sets an X-Organization response header.
Implement a Java method redirectAfterLogout(HttpServletResponse response, String returnUrl) that redirects after logout.
Implement a Java method applyClientContentType(HttpServletResponse response, String clientContentType) that sets the response content type.
Implement a Java method addNoticeHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String noticeText) that adds an X-Notice response header.
Implement a Java method setResponseHeaders(HttpHeaders headers, String sessionId) that stores the session id in an X-Session-Id header.
